Alright, Dr. Kesha here and I am so happy to inform you that the hard work has now been done. The hard work has now been done. We go over this in the fitness section where we're now out of the super hardcore dedicated fat loss phase and moving into a phase that specific activity gets incorporated. It's a very exciting time. Figure out if the person is still appropriate to take the ultimate package or if there's a forebanger that's specific to their needs. Leaning on you as a coach to determine what that is because you've worked with the client, not me, right? And they went from a food list to my Bali to using a scale to now incorporating new foods but also using the food list and using food masses, using that skill that they learned, logging into a calorie tracking application like my fitness pal to maintain their deficit but also be able to venture off the food list to give themselves a little more freedom. And so here when you meet with them, you're going to be taking the information that they've gathered over the past few weeks and you're going to basically say, you know what? Don't need to deal with the food list anymore. Although I still highly recommend it. Most of the hardcore people that are in this to win it, they'll stick on the food list regardless but at this point like they should be competent enough after tracking for about a month or six weeks, right? Depending on the method. That now you can assign a protein goal and a calorie goal. Okay. So the amount of calories that you want them to eat are still going to be the same piece of the template. Right. So if they're out of 1500 calorie template, then their protein and calories is still going to equal 1500. At this point, you can be a little more custom in terms of how much protein they consume. Excuse me. And what I recommend is that you assign their first load of protein at about 0.8 grams per pound. Okay. So if it's a 200 pound person, that's 160 grams of protein. Once you have your protein allotment, then you can fill up the rest of your calories with basically whatever you want. And a smart decision is to stick with the food list, but again, not an absolute requirement. The more of the same foods that you'd eat every day, the better results you are going to get. Scouts honor for sure. So that's basically it. They're still consuming the same amount of calories. At this point, you're now giving them a protein target. And then the rest of the calories are what I call swing calories. You can fill them in with whatever at this point, they're going to be pretty hungry because they've lost a bunch of fat. They're now starting to incorporate different types of exercise. So still heavy focus on the high fiber foods, vegetables, whole protein, things of that nature. But at this point, they pretty much have free reign and they should be competent enough to be including foreign foods. Very exciting time for everyone indeed.
